discharge_obligations.role: identify the energy density whose differentiability is required along the restricted-continuation route
supply a chain-rule, weak-derivative, or certified differentiability payload for the declared energy density
justify that the differentiated energy density pairs with the terms used in the restricted energy identity
prevent the regularity-and-differentiation group from being marked discharged until all three regularity payloads are proved or certified

positive: Records the energy-density differentiability discharge lemma target after the spatial regularity integration-by-parts discharge lemma.
Separates energy-density definition, chain-rule or weak-derivative payload, derivative pairing admissibility, and regularity-group status guarding.
Makes explicit that the next blocker is a group-level discharge gate for regularity and differentiation.
negative: Does not prove a chain rule.
Does not prove weak derivative admissibility.
Does not prove derivative pairing admissibility.
Does not discharge the regularity-and-differentiation assumption group.
Does not prove the formal restricted-continuation theorem.
Does not close the full RR theorem.
